---
title: "Create Custom Forms"
---

---
title: "Create Custom Forms"
source: "https://support.hyland.com/r/Current/Hyland-Intelligent-Document-Processing-IDP/ujz1772084897649"
section: "Hyland Intelligent Document Processing (IDP)"
---

Custom Forms enable you to customize features that support configurable look ups to external systems and configure the arrangement of fields based on document class. You can customize a class's metadata panel in the dedicated extraction UI screen.

When you create a class and add at least one field in the IDP configuration, a form model is generated for that class when the field is saved. If the class is a child class, a form model is created if either the parent class or the child class has at least one field. You can navigate to the form from the configuration UI.

Adding Fields: Adding text fields to a class will add corresponding form fields to the form model associated with the class and the form models associated with the child classes. The fields added in IDP Configuration will be added to the form model in a single column. You can change the position of the fields in the form model.

Updating Fields: Updating the name of an IDP field in configuration UI also updates the name of the corresponding form field in form model associated with the class and the form models of the child classes. IDP field descriptions are not associated with form fields and do not have any impact on form fields.

Deleting Fields: Deleting a field from a class will delete the corresponding form field from the associated form model as well as from the form models of the child classes.

Removing Inheritance From Child Classes: You can either keep or discard the fields inherited from the parent class. If you keep the inhertited fields of the child class, no changes are made to the form associated with the child class. If you delete the inherited fields of the child class, the corresponding form fields are also deleted from the child class. If the child class does not have any fields left after inheritance is removed, the child class form is also deleted.

Deleting Forms: Deleting a document class also deletes the corresponding form model. Deleting all of the fields in a class, deletes the corresponding form model if the class isn’t a child class.

Detaching All Child Classes From Parent Class: Detaching all child classes from a parent class, deletes the form fields corresponding to the inherited fields from all of the child classes.

Note:
- If you add extra form fields to a form model generated by IDP Configuration, those fields are not added to the corresponding document class in the IDP Configuration UI.
- The internal validation rules for each field set in the configuration UI is also reflected in the custom form. It is recommended that validation rules should only be modified in the configuration UI.
- You can change the layout and grouping of the fields in custom forms.

To create custom forms:

1.  On the left navigation pane of the Class page, click the relevant class for which you want to create a custom form. The right pane displays a list of suggested fields corresponding to the document class selected by you.
2.  On the right pane, next to the document class name, click the ellipsis and on the menu click Navigate to Form.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
3.  The custom form for the selected class is created under Forms and opens in the extraction UI.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
4.  Save the details.  Note: To navigate back to the configuration UI, click the name of the project under IDP > Document AI Configurations.
